stances.question: Will the prosecution be able to present sufficient evidence against Vice President Sara Duterte during her impeachment trial?
stances.side: The prosecution has failed to present substantial verified evidence warranting impeachment
stances.pole_a: The prosecution has failed to present substantial verified evidence warranting impeachment
stances.pole_b: The prosecution has presented substantial verified evidence warranting impeachment
stances.rationale: As a watchdog demanding verifiable institutional accountability, I note that sixteen days into trial the prosecution has completed evidence presentation only for one of four articles (grave threats), while critical documentary evidence for the confidential funds misuse (Article I) and unexplained wealth (Article II) remains unpresented despite court-granted subpoenas—this pattern suggests either i
stances.resolves_when: 2026-09-30 or when Senate renders verdict on Articles I-IV

research.key_finding: As of July 22, 2026, the prosecution has completed evidence presentation only for Article IV (grave threats), centered on NBI Director Matibag's testimony establishing a "pattern of violence" and alleged conspiracy, while evidence for Artic

article.why: Documentary gap between subpoenaed evidence and presented material requires itemized timeline with case numbers

video.why: Evidentiary deficiency argument needs specificity that spoken format cannot deliver credibly
